content.content:
1→# Forge
     2→
     3→AI-powered development orchestrator that breaks specs into phases and runs Claude iteratively until completion. Forge embodies disciplined agentic development with calm gating, thoughtful planning, optimized resource usage, and efficient execution.
     4→
     5→## Overview
     6→
     7→Forge transforms a project specification into executable phases, running Claude AI against each phase with structured prompts. It monitors for completion signals, manages context to prevent overflow, tracks file changes, provides intelligent approval gates, and maintains comprehensive audit logs.
     8→
     9→## Features
    10→
    11→- **Phase-Based Execution**: Break complex projects into sequential phases with iteration budgets
    12→- **Hook System**: Event-driven extensibility with command and prompt hooks
    13→- **Skills/Templates**: Reusable prompt fragments loaded on-demand
    14→- **Context Compaction**: Automatic summarization to prevent context overflow
    15→- **Sub-Phase Delegation**: Spawn child phases for scope discovery
    16→- **Permission Modes**: Varying oversight levels (strict, standard, autonomous, readonly)
    17→- **Pattern Learning**: Capture and reuse patterns from successful projects
    18→- **Progress Signaling**: Intermediate status updates, blockers, and pivots
    19→
    20→## Installation
    21→
    22→### Prerequisites
    23→
    24→- Rust toolchain (Edition 2024)
    25→- Git
    26→- [Claude CLI](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) installed and in PATH
    27→
    28→### Build
    29→
    30→```bash
    31→cargo build --release
    32→```
    33→
    34→### Install
    35→
    36→```bash
    37→# Option 1: Install via cargo
    38→cargo install --path .
    39→
    40→# Option 2: Symlink to PATH
    41→ln -s $(pwd)/target/release/forge ~/.local/bin/forge
    42→```
    43→
    44→## Quick Start
    45→
    46→```bash
    47→# Initialize a new project
    48→forge init
    49→
    50→# Create a spec interactively
    51→forge interview
    52→
    53→# Generate phases from the spec
    54→forge generate
    55→
    56→# Start the orchestration loop
    57→forge run
    58→
    59→# View phases
    60→forge list
    61→
    62→# Check progress
    63→forge status
    64→```
    65→
    66→## Commands
    67→
    68→### Core Workflow
    69→
    70→| Command | Description |
    71→|---------|-------------|
    72→| `forge init` | Initialize `.forge/` directory structure |
    73→| `forge interview` | Interactive spec generation |
    74→| `forge generate` | Create phases from spec |
    75→| `forge run` | Execute phases sequentially |
    76→| `forge run --phase 07` | Start from specific phase |
    77→| `forge phase <N>` | Run a single phase |
    78→| `forge list` | Display all phases |
    79→| `forge status` | Show progress |
    80→| `forge reset` | Reset all progress |
    81→| `forge factory` | Launch the Factory Kanban board UI |
    82→
    83→### Configuration
    84→
    85→| Command | Description |
    86→|---------|-------------|
    87→| `forge config show` | Display current configuration |
    88→| `forge config validate` | Check configuration for issues |
    89→| `forge config init` | Create default `forge.toml` |
    90→
    91→### Skills Management
    92→
    93→| Command | Description |
    94→|---------|-------------|
    95→| `forge skills` | List all available skills |
    96→| `forge skills show <name>` | Display skill content |
    97→| `forge skills create <name>` | Create a new skill |
    98→| `forge skills delete <name>` | Delete a skill |
    99→
   100→### Pattern Learning
   101→
   102→| Command | Description |
   103→|---------|-------------|
   104→| `forge learn` | Learn pattern from current project |
   105→| `forge patterns` | List learned patterns |
   106→| `forge patterns show <name>` | Show pattern details |
   107→| `forge patterns stats` | Aggregate statistics |
   108→| `forge patterns recommend` | Suggest patterns for spec |
   109→| `forge patterns compare` | Compare two patterns |
   110→
   111→### Context Management
   112→
   113→| Command | Description |
   114→|---------|-------------|
   115→| `forge compact` | Manually trigger compaction |
   116→| `forge compact --status` | Show compaction status |
   117→
   118→### Audit
   119→
   120→| Command | Description |
   121→|---------|-------------|
   122→| `forge audit show <phase>` | View phase audit |
   123→| `forge audit changes` | Show file changes |
   124→| `forge audit export <file>` | Export audit to JSON |
   125→
   126→## Global Options
   127→
   128→| Option | Description |
   129→|--------|-------------|
   130→| `-v, --verbose` | Enable verbose output |
   131→| `--yes` | Auto-approve all phases |
   132→| `--auto-approve-threshold <N>` | Auto-approve when file changes ≤ N (default: 5) |
   133→| `--project-dir <PATH>` | Project directory |
   134→| `--spec-file <PATH>` | Path to spec file |
   135→| `--context-limit <LIMIT>` | Context limit (e.g., "80%" or "500000") |
   136→
   137→## Configuration
   138→
   139→### forge.toml
   140→
   141→Create `.forge/forge.toml` for project configuration:
   142→
   143→```toml
   144→[project]
   145→name = "my-project"
   146→claude_cmd = "claude"
   147→
   148→[defaults]
   149→budget = 8
   150→auto_approve_threshold = 5
   151→permission_mode = "standard"  # strict, standard, autonomous, readonly
   152→context_limit = "80%"
   153→skip_permissions = true
   154→
   155→# Phase-specific overrides using glob patterns
   156→[phases.overrides."database-*"]
   157→permission_mode = "strict"
   158→budget = 12
   159→
   160→[phases.overrides."*-readonly"]
   161→permission_mode = "readonly"
   162→
   163→# Global skills (loaded for all phases)
   164→[skills]
   165→global = ["rust-conventions"]
   166→
   167→# Hook definitions
   168→[[hooks.definitions]]
   169→event = "PrePhase"
   170→match_pattern = "*database*"
   171→command = "./scripts/ensure-db.sh"
   172→
   173→[[hooks.definitions]]
   174→event = "OnApproval"
   175→type = "prompt"
   176→prompt = "Should we proceed? Return {approve: bool, reason: str}"
   177→```
   178→
   179→### Environment Variables
   180→
   181→| Variable | Description | Default |
   182→|----------|-------------|---------|
   183→| `CLAUDE_CMD` | Claude CLI command | `claude` |
   184→| `SKIP_PERMISSIONS` | Skip permission prompts | `true` |
   185→| `FORGE_CMD` | Forge CLI command for pipeline execution | `forge` |
   186→
   187→## Hook System
   188→
   189→Hooks intercept lifecycle events for custom behavior:
   190→
   191→### Hook Events
   192→
   193→| Event | Description |
   194→|-------|-------------|
   195→| `PrePhase` | Before phase execution |
   196→| `PostPhase` | After phase completion |
   197→| `PreIteration` | Before each Claude invocation |
   198→| `PostIteration` | After each Claude response |
   199→| `OnFailure` | When phase exceeds budget |
   200→| `OnApproval` | When approval gate is presented |
   201→
   202→### Hook Types
   203→
   204→**Command Hooks**: Execute bash scripts with JSON input
   205→
   206→```toml
   207→[[hooks.definitions]]
   208→event = "PrePhase"
   209→command = "./scripts/setup.sh"
   210→```
   211→
   212→**Prompt Hooks**: Use LLM to evaluate conditions
   213→
   214→```toml
   215→[[hooks.definitions]]
   216→event = "PostIteration"
   217→type = "prompt"
   218→prompt = "Did Claude make meaningful progress? Return {continue: bool}"
   219→```
   220→
   221→## Orchestrator Lifecycle
   222→
   223→Each `forge run` invocation moves through a well-defined lifecycle for every phase.
   224→Understanding this lifecycle is essential when writing hooks, debugging failures, or reading logs.
   225→
   226→### Phase Lifecycle
   227→
   228→```
   229→forge run
   230→  └─ For each phase (sequential) or wave of phases (DAG/swarm):
   231→       1. ApprovalGate.check_phase()          ← GateDecision: Approved | Rejected | Aborted
   232→       2. Hook: PrePhase                       ← runs before any iteration starts
   233→       3. For each iteration (1 → budget):
   234→            a. Hook: PreIteration
   235→            b. Claude invocation (streaming)
   236→            c. Parse output for:
   237→               - <promise>…</promise>          ← phase completion signal
   238→               - <progress>N%</progress>       ← updates UI progress bar
   239→               - <blocker>…</blocker>          ← displayed to operator
   240→               - <pivot>…</pivot>              ← injected into next iteration prompt
   241→               - <spawn_subphase>{…}           ← triggers sub-phase
   242→            d. Collect git diff (file changes)
   243→            e. Hook: PostIteration
   244→            f. If promise found → break (phase complete)
   245→            g. If budget exhausted → Hook: OnFailure → phase fails
   246→       4. Hook: PostPhase
   247→       5. (If swarm) Hook: OnApproval for review specialists
   248→```
   249→
   250→### Hook Invocation Order
   251→
   252→| Event | When | Typical use |
   253→|-------|------|-------------|
   254→| `PrePhase` | After gate approval, before iteration 1 | Set up databases, fetch secrets |
   255→| `PostPhase` | After all iterations complete | Run test suite, notify Slack |
   256→| `PreIteration` | Before each Claude invocation | Inject context, update prompts |
   257→| `PostIteration` | After each Claude response is parsed | Log token usage, trigger CI |
   258→| `OnFailure` | When budget is exhausted without a promise | Alert on-call, open incident |
   259→| `OnApproval` | When the approval gate presents a prompt | LLM-assisted decision making |
   260→
   261→### Permission Mode Lifecycle Differences
   262→
   263→| Mode | Phase approval | Iteration approval | File writes |
   264→|------|---------------|-------------------|-------------|
   265→| `strict` | Interactive prompt | Interactive prompt | Allowed |
   266→| `standard` | Auto if ≤ threshold file changes | Auto-continue | Allowed |
   267→| `autonomous` | Auto-approve | Auto-continue; prompt if 3+ stale iters | Allowed |
   268→| `readonly` | Auto-approve | Auto-continue | **Blocked** |
   269→
   270→### Promise Detection
   271→
   272→Claude signals phase completion by emitting:
   273→
   274→```xml
   275→<promise>DONE</promise>
   276→```
   277→
   278→The token inside the tags must match the phase's configured `promise` string (case-sensitive). Once detected, the orchestrator stops iterations and advances to the next phase.
   279→
   280→## Skills System
   281→
   282→Skills are reusable prompt fragments in `.forge/skills/`:
   283→
   284→```
   285→.forge/skills/
   286→├── rust-conventions/
   287→│   └── SKILL.md
   288→├── testing-strategy/
   289→│   └── SKILL.md
   290→└── api-design/
   291→    └── SKILL.md
   292→```
   293→
   294→Reference skills in phases:
   295→
   296→```json
   297→{
   298→  "number": "03",
   299→  "name": "API implementation",
   300→  "skills": ["rust-conventions", "api-design"],
   301→  "promise": "API COMPLETE"
   302→}
   303→```
   304→
   305→## Permission Modes
   306→
   307→| Mode | Description |
   308→|------|-------------|
   309→| `strict` | Require approval for every iteration |
   310→| `standard` | Approve phase start, auto-continue iterations |
   311→| `autonomous` | Auto-approve if within budget and making progress |
   312→| `readonly` | Research/planning only, no file modifications |
   313→
   314→## Progress Signaling
   315→
   316→Beyond binary promise detection, Claude can output intermediate signals:
   317→
   318→```xml
   319→<progress>50%</progress>     <!-- Partial completion -->
   320→<blocker>Need X</blocker>    <!-- Pause and prompt user -->
   321→<pivot>New approach Y</pivot> <!-- Strategy shift logged -->
   322→```
   323→
   324→## Sub-Phase Delegation
   325→
   326→Phases can spawn child phases for discovered scope:
   327→
   328→```xml
   329→<spawn_subphase>
   330→{
   331→  "name": "Set up OAuth provider",
   332→  "promise": "OAUTH COMPLETE",
   333→  "budget": 5,
   334→  "reasoning": "OAuth setup is complex enough for its own phase"
   335→}
   336→</spawn_subphase>
   337→```
   338→
   339→Sub-phases:
   340→- Inherit parent's git snapshot as baseline
   341→- Have independent budgets carved from parent
   342→- Must complete before parent can complete
   343→- Maintain hierarchical audit trail
   344→
   345→## Context Compaction
   346→
   347→Forge tracks context usage and automatically summarizes prior iterations when approaching limits:
   348→
   349→- Preserves: current phase goal, recent code changes, error context
   350→- Discards: verbose intermediate outputs, superseded attempts
   351→- Configurable threshold via `--context-limit` or `forge.toml`
   352→
   353→## Pattern Learning
   354→
   355→Capture project patterns for future use:
   356→
   357→```bash
   358→# After completing a project
   359→forge learn --name my-pattern
   360→
   361→# Apply to new projects
   362→forge patterns recommend --spec ./new-project-spec.md
   363→```
   364→
   365→Patterns capture:
   366→- Phase type classification (scaffold, implement, test, refactor, fix)
   367→- Typical iteration counts by phase type
   368→- Common failure modes and recovery patterns
   369→- File change patterns
   370→- Effective prompt structures
   371→
   372→## Directory Structure
   373→
   374→### Project Directory
   375→
   376→```
   377→.forge/
   378→├── forge.toml       # Configuration (optional)
   379→├── hooks.toml       # Hooks (optional, can also be in forge.toml)
   380→├── spec.md          # Project specification
   381→├── phases.json      # Generated phases with dependencies
   382→├── state            # Execution state (append-only)
   383→├── checkpoints/     # Swarm checkpoint files for recovery
   384→├── audit/
   385→│   ├── runs/        # Completed run logs (JSON)
   386→│   └── current-run.json
   387→├── logs/            # Phase prompts and outputs
   388→├── prompts/         # Custom prompt overrides
   389→└── skills/          # Reusable prompt fragments
   390→    ├── skill-name/
   391→    │   └── SKILL.md
   392→    └── ...
   393→```
   394→
   395→### Source Code Structure
   396→
   397→```
   398→src/
   399→├── main.rs              # CLI entry point
   400→├── lib.rs               # Library exports
   401→├── phase.rs             # Phase definitions
   402→├── forge_config.rs      # Configuration parsing
   403→│
   404→├── orchestrator/        # Core orchestration
   405→│   ├── runner.rs        # Phase execution loop
   406→│   ├── state.rs         # State persistence
   407→│   └── review_integration.rs
   408→│
   409→├── dag/                 # DAG scheduler (swarm)
   410→│   ├── builder.rs       # Graph construction
   411→│   ├── scheduler.rs     # Wave computation
   412→│   ├── executor.rs      # Parallel dispatch
   413→│   └── state.rs         # Execution tracking
   414→│
   415→├── swarm/               # Swarm integration
   416→│   ├── executor.rs      # Swarm orchestration
   417→│   ├── context.rs       # Swarm types
   418→│   ├── callback.rs      # HTTP callback server
   419→│   └── prompts.rs       # Orchestration prompts
   420→│
   421→├── review/              # Review system
   422→│   ├── specialists.rs   # Specialist definitions
   423→│   ├── dispatcher.rs    # Review coordination
   424→│   ├── arbiter.rs       # LLM resolution
   425→│   └── findings.rs      # Finding types
   426→│
   427→├── decomposition/       # Dynamic decomposition
   428→│   ├── config.rs        # Decomposition configuration
   429→│   ├── detector.rs      # Complexity detection
   430→│   ├── parser.rs        # Decomposition parsing
   431→│   ├── executor.rs      # Sub-task execution
   432→│   └── types.rs         # Decomposition types
   433→│
   434→├── factory/             # Factory Kanban board
   435→│   ├── api.rs           # REST API handlers (Axum)
   436→│   ├── server.rs        # Server startup & config
   437→│   ├── db.rs            # SQLite database layer
   438→│   ├── models.rs        # Data models & view types
   439→│   ├── pipeline.rs      # Pipeline execution engine
   440→│   └── ws.rs            # WebSocket message types
   441→│
   442→├── hooks/               # Hook system
   443→├── skills/              # Skills management
   444→├── context/             # Context management
   445→├── tracker/             # Git operations
   446→└── ui/                  # Progress display
   447→```
   448→
   449→## Example Session
   450→
   451→```bash
   452→$ forge init
   453→Initialized forge project at .forge/
   454→
   455→$ forge interview
   456→# Interactive Q&A to generate spec...
   457→Spec saved to .forge/spec.md
   458→
   459→$ forge generate
   460→Analyzing spec...
   461→Generated 10 phases
   462→
   463→$ forge run
   464→[Phase 01/10] Project scaffolding
   465→  Promise: SCAFFOLD COMPLETE | Budget: 8 iterations | Mode: standard
   466→
   467→? Approve phase 01?
   468→❯ Yes, run this phase
   469→  Yes, and auto-approve remaining
   470→  Skip this phase
   471→  Abort
   472→
   473→[Iteration 1/8] Running Claude...
   474→  Context: 45% used
   475→  Files: +15 added, 2 modified
   476→✅ Promise found! Phase 01 complete.
   477→
   478→[Phase 02/10] Core implementation
   479→  Loading skills: rust-conventions
   480→...
   481→
   482→$ forge status
   483→Forge Project Status
   484→====================
   485→Project: Initialized
   486→Spec:    Ready
   487→Phases:  Ready (10 phases)
   488→
   489→Execution Progress:
   490→  Phases completed: 5
   491→  Last completed: 05
   492→
   493→$ forge patterns
   494→No patterns found. Complete a project and run 'forge learn'.
   495→```
   496→
   497→## Swarm Orchestration
   498→
   499→Forge supports parallel phase execution through a hybrid swarm architecture that combines native Rust DAG scheduling with Claude Code's agent coordination capabilities.
   500→
   501→### Overview
   502→
   503→The swarm system provides:
   504→
   505→- **Parallel Phase Execution**: Run independent phases simultaneously via DAG scheduling
   506→- **Review Specialists**: Automated code review with security, performance, and architecture analysis
   507→- **Dynamic Decomposition**: Automatically split complex phases into smaller tasks
   508→- **LLM Arbiter**: Autonomous resolution of review failures without human intervention
   509→- **Checkpoint Recovery**: Resume interrupted runs from saved state
   510→
   511→### Swarm Commands
   512→
   513→| Command | Description |
   514→|---------|-------------|
   515→| `forge swarm` | Execute phases in parallel using DAG scheduler |
   516→| `forge swarm --from 05` | Start parallel execution from phase 05 |
   517→| `forge swarm --only 07` | Run only phase 07 |
   518→| `forge swarm status` | Show current swarm execution status |
   519→| `forge swarm abort` | Gracefully stop running swarm |
   520→
   521→### Swarm Options
   522→
   523→| Option | Description | Default |
   524→|--------|-------------|---------|
   525→| `--max-parallel <N>` | Maximum concurrent phases | 4 |
   526→| `--backend <TYPE>` | Execution backend: auto, in-process, tmux, iterm2 | auto |
   527→| `--review <SPECIALISTS>` | Enable review: security, performance, architecture, simplicity, all | none |
   528→| `--review-mode <MODE>` | Resolution mode: manual, auto, arbiter | manual |
   529→| `--max-fix-attempts <N>` | Maximum auto-fix attempts | 2 |
   530→| `--escalate-on <TYPES>` | Always escalate these finding types (comma-separated) | none |
   531→| `--arbiter-confidence <N>` | Minimum confidence for arbiter (0.0-1.0) | 0.7 |
   532→| `--decompose` | Enable dynamic decomposition | true |
   533→| `--no-decompose` | Disable dynamic decomposition | |
   534→| `--decompose-threshold <N>` | Budget percentage to trigger decomposition | 50 |
   535→| `--permission-mode <MODE>` | Permission mode: strict, standard, autonomous | standard |
   536→| `--ui <MODE>` | Output format: full, minimal, json | full |
   537→| `--fail-fast` | Stop all phases on first failure | disabled |
   538→
   539→### How DAG Scheduling Works
   540→
   541→Forge analyzes phase dependencies and computes execution waves:
   542→
   543→```
   544→phases.json with dependencies:
   545→  01 (no deps) ─┐
   546→  02 → [01]     │ Wave 1: [01]
   547→  03 → [01]     ├ Wave 2: [02, 03, 04]
   548→  04 → [01]     │ Wave 3: [05, 06]
   549→  05 → [02, 03] │ Wave 4: [07]
   550→  06 → [03, 04] │
   551→  07 → [05, 06] ┘
   552→
   553→Phases in the same wave run in parallel (up to --max-parallel).
   554→```
   555→
   556→### Phase Dependencies
   557→
   558→Define dependencies in `phases.json`:
   559→
   560→```json
   561→{
   562→  "number": "05",
   563→  "name": "OAuth integration",
   564→  "promise": "OAUTH COMPLETE",
   565→  "budget": 10,
   566→  "depends_on": ["02", "03"]
   567→}
   568→```
   569→
   570→### Review Specialists
   571→
   572→Review specialists automatically analyze completed phases:
   573→
   574→| Specialist | Focus Areas | Gate |
   575→|------------|-------------|------|
   576→| `security` | Injection risks, auth issues, secrets exposure | Yes |
   577→| `performance` | N+1 queries, memory leaks, algorithmic complexity | No |
   578→| `architecture` | SOLID violations, coupling, separation of concerns | Yes |
   579→| `simplicity` | Over-engineering, premature abstraction, YAGNI | No |
   580→
   581→**Gating reviews** block phase completion until issues are resolved.
   582→**Advisory reviews** report findings but don't block progress.
   583→
   584→Enable reviews:
   585→
   586→```bash
   587→# Single specialist
   588→forge swarm --review security
   589→
   590→# Multiple specialists
   591→forge swarm --review security,performance
   592→
   593→# All specialists
   594→forge swarm --review all
   595→```
   596→
   597→### Review Output
   598→
   599→```json
   600→{
   601→  "phase": "05",
   602→  "reviewer": "security-sentinel",
   603→  "verdict": "warn",
   604→  "findings": [
   605→    {
   606→      "severity": "warning",
   607→      "file": "src/auth/oauth.rs",
   608→      "line": 142,
   609→      "issue": "Token stored in localStorage is vulnerable to XSS",
   610→      "suggestion": "Use httpOnly cookies instead"
   611→    }
   612→  ]
   613→}
   614→```
   615→
   616→### Resolution Modes
   617→
   618→When a gating review fails, three resolution modes are available:
   619→
   620→| Mode | Behavior |
   621→|------|----------|
   622→| `manual` | Always pause for user input |
   623→| `auto` | Attempt auto-fix, retry up to 2 times |
   624→| `arbiter` | LLM decides based on severity and context |
   625→
   626→The **arbiter** mode uses an LLM to analyze findings and decide:
   627→
   628→- **PROCEED**: Continue despite findings (style issues, false positives)
   629→- **FIX**: Spawn fix agent and retry (clear fix path exists)
   630→- **ESCALATE**: Require human decision (architectural concerns)
   631→
   632→```bash
   633→# Use arbiter with 80% confidence threshold
   634→forge swarm --review security --review-mode arbiter --arbiter-confidence 0.8
   635→```
   636→
   637→### Dynamic Decomposition
   638→
   639→When a phase is too complex, Forge can automatically decompose it:
   640→
   641→**Triggers:**
   642→- Worker emits `<blocker>` with complexity signal
   643→- Iterations exceed 50% budget with progress < 30%
   644→- Worker requests: `<request-decomposition/>`
   645→
   646→**Example decomposition:**
   647→
   648→```
   649→Phase 05: OAuth Integration (budget: 20)
   650→         │
   651→         │ Detected: "3 separate provider integrations needed"
   652→         ▼
   653→┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
   654→│ Decomposition produces:                │
   655→│ ├── 05.1: Google OAuth (budget: 5) ─┐  │
   656→│ ├── 05.2: GitHub OAuth (budget: 5) ─┼─ parallel
   657→│ ├── 05.3: Auth0 OAuth  (budget: 5) ─┘  │
   658→│ └── 05.4: Unified handler (budget: 3)  │
   659→│           depends_on: [05.1-3]         │
   660→└────────────────────────────────────────┘
   661→```
   662→
   663→### Swarm Configuration
   664→
   665→Configure swarm behavior in `forge.toml`:
   666→
   667→```toml
   668→[swarm]
   669→enabled = true
   670→backend = "auto"                    # auto, in-process, tmux, iterm2
   671→default_strategy = "adaptive"
   672→max_agents = 5
   673→
   674→[swarm.reviews]
   675→enabled = true
   676→specialists = ["security", "performance"]
   677→mode = "arbiter"                    # manual, auto, arbiter
   678→
   679→# Per-phase swarm overrides
   680→[phases.overrides."*-complex"]
   681→swarm = { strategy = "parallel", max_agents = 4 }
   682→
   683→[phases.overrides."*-refactor"]
   684→swarm = { strategy = "wave_pipeline", reviews = ["architecture"] }
   685→```
   686→
   687→### Per-Phase Swarm Config
   688→
   689→Enable swarm execution for specific phases:
   690→
   691→```json
   692→{
   693→  "number": "05",
   694→  "name": "OAuth integration",
   695→  "promise": "OAUTH COMPLETE",
   696→  "budget": 15,
   697→  "swarm": {
   698→    "strategy": "parallel",
   699→    "max_agents": 4,
   700→    "reviews": ["security"]
   701→  }
   702→}
   703→```
   704→
   705→### Checkpoint & Recovery
   706→
   707→Forge automatically checkpoints progress during swarm execution:
   708→
   709→```bash
   710→# Resume interrupted run
   711→$ forge swarm
   712→Detected incomplete run: forge-run-20260126-150322
   713→  Progress: 14/22 phases
   714→  Checkpoints: 3 phases resumable
   715→
   716→Resume? [Y/n] y
   717→
   718→Recovering...
   719→  ✓ Loaded checkpoints
   720→  ✓ Reconciled state
   721→  ✓ Respawning 3 phases
   722→
   723→Continuing from Wave 4...
   724→```
   725→
   726→### Example Swarm Session
   727→
   728→```bash
   729→$ forge swarm --review security --max-parallel 3
   730→
   731→Analyzing phase dependencies...
   732→  22 phases, 8 execution waves
   733→
   734→Wave 1: [01] ████████████ 100%  (3 iterations)
   735→
   736→Wave 2: [02] ████████░░░░  67%
   737→        [03] ████████████ 100%
   738→        [06] ████████████ 100%
   739→
   740→Reviews for [03]:
   741→  ✓ security-sentinel: PASS
   742→
   743→Wave 2: [02] ████████████ 100%  (10 iterations)
   744→
   745→Reviews for [02]:
   746→  ⚠ security-sentinel: WARN (1 finding)
   747→    └─ src/db/queries.rs:42 - Consider parameterized query
   748→
   749→Continuing (non-gating)...
   750→
   751→Wave 3: [04] ████░░░░░░░░  33%
   752→        [05*] Starting swarm...
   753→              └─ Spawning 3 agents for OAuth integration
   754→        [07] ████████████ 100%
   755→
   756→All phases complete!
   757→  Total time: 12m 34s
   758→  Phases: 22/22
   759→  Reviews: 8 passed, 1 warning
   760→```
   761→
   762→### Troubleshooting
   763→
   764→#### Swarm won't start
   765→
   766→```
   767→Error: No phases.json found
   768→```
   769→
   770→Run `forge generate` first to create phases from your spec.
   771→
   772→#### Phase stuck in "Blocked" state
   773→
   774→Check dependencies with `forge list --deps`. A phase stays blocked until all its dependencies complete successfully.
   775→
   776→#### Review keeps failing
   777→
   778→1. Check the review findings in the output
   779→2. Try `--review-mode auto` to attempt automatic fixes
   780→3. Use `--review-mode arbiter` for LLM-assisted resolution
   781→4. Lower the gating threshold or remove the specialist from gating
   782→
   783→#### Out of memory during parallel execution
   784→
   785→Reduce `--max-parallel` to limit concurrent Claude instances:
   786→
   787→```bash
   788→forge swarm --max-parallel 2
   789→```
   790→
   791→#### Checkpoint recovery fails
   792→
   793→Clear checkpoints and restart:
   794→
   795→```bash
   796→rm -rf .forge/checkpoints/
   797→forge swarm
   798→```
   799→
   800→## Factory (Kanban Board)
   801→
   802→Forge Factory is a web-based Kanban board for managing issues that can self-implement through the Forge orchestration engine. Launch it with:
   803→
   804→```bash
   805→forge factory
   806→```
   807→
   808→This starts a local web server (default port 3000) with a drag-and-drop Kanban board and real-time pipeline progress.
   809→
   810→### Kanban Columns
   811→
   812→| Column | Description |
   813→|--------|-------------|
   814→| **Backlog** | New issues waiting to be worked on |
   815→| **Ready** | Issues prioritized and ready for pipeline execution |
   816→| **In Progress** | Issues currently being implemented by a pipeline |
   817→| **In Review** | Pipeline completed, PR created and awaiting review |
   818→| **Done** | Issue resolved and merged |
   819→
   820→Issues can be dragged between columns manually, or they move automatically during pipeline execution.
   821→
   822→### Self-Implementing Issues
   823→
   824→Clicking **"Run Pipeline"** on an issue triggers the full implementation lifecycle:
   825→
   826→1. **Column moves to In Progress** — issue is picked up
   827→2. **Git branch created** — `forge/issue-{id}-{slug}` branch for isolation
   828→3. **Phases auto-generated** — issue description written to `.forge/spec.md`, then `forge generate` creates `phases.json`
   829→4. **Pipeline executes** — `forge swarm` runs if phases exist, otherwise falls back to `claude --print` for simple issues
   830→5. **PR created** — on success, a pull request is opened via `gh pr create`
   831→6. **Column moves to In Review** — signals the work is ready for human review
   832→
   833→### Real-Time Progress
   834→
   835→The Factory UI receives live updates via WebSocket:
   836→
   837→| Event | Description |
   838→|-------|-------------|
   839→| `PipelineStarted` | Pipeline execution begins |
   840→| `PipelineBranchCreated` | Git branch created for the issue |
   841→| `PipelinePhaseStarted` | A DAG phase begins execution |
   842→| `PipelinePhaseCompleted` | A DAG phase finishes |
   843→| `PipelineReviewStarted` | Code review begins for a phase |
   844→| `PipelineReviewCompleted` | Code review finishes with findings |
   845→| `PipelinePrCreated` | Pull request created on success |
   846→| `PipelineCompleted` | Pipeline finishes (success or failure) |
   847→
   848→The issue detail panel shows a **Phase Timeline** with per-phase status, iteration progress, and duration.
   849→
   850→### Factory Options
   851→
   852→| Option | Description | Default |
   853→|--------|-------------|---------|
   854→| `--port <PORT>` | HTTP server port | `3000` |
   855→| `--db-path <PATH>` | SQLite database file | `.forge/factory.db` |
   856→
   857→### Factory REST API
   858→
   859→| Method | Endpoint | Description |
   860→|--------|----------|-------------|
   861→| `GET` | `/api/board` | Get board view with all columns and issues |
   862→| `POST` | `/api/issues` | Create a new issue |
   863→| `GET` | `/api/issues/:id` | Get issue detail with pipeline runs and phases |
   864→| `PUT` | `/api/issues/:id` | Update an issue |
   865→| `DELETE` | `/api/issues/:id` | Delete an issue |
   866→| `PUT` | `/api/issues/:id/move` | Move issue to a different column |
   867→| `POST` | `/api/issues/:id/pipeline` | Trigger pipeline execution |
   868→| `POST` | `/api/pipeline-runs/:id/cancel` | Cancel a running pipeline |
   869→| `GET` | `/ws` | WebSocket endpoint for real-time updates |
   870→
   871→## Testing
   872→
   873→```bash
   874→# Run all tests
   875→cargo test
   876→
   877→# Unit tests only
   878→cargo test --lib
   879→
   880→# Integration tests only
   881→cargo test --test integration_tests
   882→```
   883→
   884→## License
   885→
   886→MIT
   887→

file.content:
# Forge

AI-powered development orchestrator that breaks specs into phases and runs Claude iteratively until completion. Forge embodies disciplined agentic development with calm gating, thoughtful planning, optimized resource usage, and efficient execution.

## Overview

Forge transforms a project specification into executable phases, running Claude AI against each phase with structured prompts. It monitors for completion signals, manages context to prevent overflow, tracks file changes, provides intelligent approval gates, and maintains comprehensive audit logs.

## Features

- **Phase-Based Execution**: Break complex projects into sequential phases with iteration budgets
- **Hook System**: Event-driven extensibility with command and prompt hooks
- **Skills/Templates**: Reusable prompt fragments loaded on-demand
- **Context Compaction**: Automatic summarization to prevent context overflow
- **Sub-Phase Delegation**: Spawn child phases for scope discovery
- **Permission Modes**: Varying oversight levels (strict, standard, autonomous, readonly)
- **Pattern Learning**: Capture and reuse patterns from successful projects
- **Progress Signaling**: Intermediate status updates, blockers, and pivots

## Installation

### Prerequisites

- Rust toolchain (Edition 2024)
- Git
- [Claude CLI](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) installed and in PATH

### Build

```bash
cargo build --release
```

### Install

```bash
# Option 1: Install via cargo
cargo install --path .

# Option 2: Symlink to PATH
ln -s $(pwd)/target/release/forge ~/.local/bin/forge
```

## Quick Start

```bash
# Initialize a new project
forge init

# Create a spec interactively
forge interview

# Generate phases from the spec
forge generate

# Start the orchestration loop
forge run

# View phases
forge list

# Check progress
forge status
```

## Commands

### Core Workflow

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `forge init` | Initialize `.forge/` directory structure |
| `forge interview` | Interactive spec generation |
| `forge generate` | Create phases from spec |
| `forge run` | Execute phases sequentially |
| `forge run --phase 07` | Start from specific phase |
| `forge phase <N>` | Run a single phase |
| `forge list` | Display all phases |
| `forge status` | Show progress |
| `forge reset` | Reset all progress |
| `forge factory` | Launch the Factory Kanban board UI |

### Configuration

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `forge config show` | Display current configuration |
| `forge config validate` | Check configuration for issues |
| `forge config init` | Create default `forge.toml` |

### Skills Management

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `forge skills` | List all available skills |
| `forge skills show <name>` | Display skill content |
| `forge skills create <name>` | Create a new skill |
| `forge skills delete <name>` | Delete a skill |

### Pattern Learning

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `forge learn` | Learn pattern from current project |
| `forge patterns` | List learned patterns |
| `forge patterns show <name>` | Show pattern details |
| `forge patterns stats` | Aggregate statistics |
| `forge patterns recommend` | Suggest patterns for spec |
| `forge patterns compare` | Compare two patterns |

### Context Management

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `forge compact` | Manually trigger compaction |
| `forge compact --status` | Show compaction status |

### Audit

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `forge audit show <phase>` | View phase audit |
| `forge audit changes` | Show file changes |
| `forge audit export <file>` | Export audit to JSON |

## Global Options

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `-v, --verbose` | Enable verbose output |
| `--yes` | Auto-approve all phases |
| `--auto-approve-threshold <N>` | Auto-approve when file changes ≤ N (default: 5) |
| `--project-dir <PATH>` | Project directory |
| `--spec-file <PATH>` | Path to spec file |
| `--context-limit <LIMIT>` | Context limit (e.g., "80%" or "500000") |

## Configuration

### forge.toml

Create `.forge/forge.toml` for project configuration:

```toml
[project]
name = "my-project"
claude_cmd = "claude"

[defaults]
budget = 8
auto_approve_threshold = 5
permission_mode = "standard"  # strict, standard, autonomous, readonly
context_limit = "80%"
skip_permissions = true

# Phase-specific overrides using glob patterns
[phases.overrides."database-*"]
permission_mode = "strict"
budget = 12

[phases.overrides."*-readonly"]
permission_mode = "readonly"

# Global skills (loaded for all phases)
[skills]
global = ["rust-conventions"]

# Hook definitions
[[hooks.definitions]]
event = "PrePhase"
match_pattern = "*database*"
command = "./scripts/ensure-db.sh"

[[hooks.definitions]]
event = "OnApproval"
type = "prompt"
prompt = "Should we proceed? Return {approve: bool, reason: str}"
```

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Description | Default |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| `CLAUDE_CMD` | Claude CLI command | `claude` |
| `SKIP_PERMISSIONS` | Skip permission prompts | `true` |
| `FORGE_CMD` | Forge CLI command for pipeline execution | `forge` |

## Hook System

Hooks intercept lifecycle events for custom behavior:

### Hook Events

| Event | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `PrePhase` | Before phase execution |
| `PostPhase` | After phase completion |
| `PreIteration` | Before each Claude invocation |
| `PostIteration` | After each Claude response |
| `OnFailure` | When phase exceeds budget |
| `OnApproval` | When approval gate is presented |

### Hook Types

**Command Hooks**: Execute bash scripts with JSON input

```toml
[[hooks.definitions]]
event = "PrePhase"
command = "./scripts/setup.sh"
```

**Prompt Hooks**: Use LLM to evaluate conditions

```toml
[[hooks.definitions]]
event = "PostIteration"
type = "prompt"
prompt = "Did Claude make meaningful progress? Return {continue: bool}"
```

## Orchestrator Lifecycle

Each `forge run` invocation moves through a well-defined lifecycle for every phase.
Understanding this lifecycle is essential when writing hooks, debugging failures, or reading logs.

### Phase Lifecycle

```
forge run
  └─ For each phase (sequential) or wave of phases (DAG/swarm):
       1. ApprovalGate.check_phase()          ← GateDecision: Approved | Rejected | Aborted
       2. Hook: PrePhase                       ← runs before any iteration starts
       3. For each iteration (1 → budget):
            a. Hook: PreIteration
            b. Claude invocation (streaming)
            c. Parse output for:
               - <promise>…</promise>          ← phase completion signal
               - <progress>N%</progress>       ← updates UI progress bar
               - <blocker>…</blocker>          ← displayed to operator
               - <pivot>…</pivot>              ← injected into next iteration prompt
               - <spawn_subphase>{…}           ← triggers sub-phase
            d. Collect git diff (file changes)
            e. Hook: PostIteration
            f. If promise found → break (phase complete)
            g. If budget exhausted → Hook: OnFailure → phase fails
       4. Hook: PostPhase
       5. (If swarm) Hook: OnApproval for review specialists
```

### Hook Invocation Order

| Event | When | Typical use |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `PrePhase` | After gate approval, before iteration 1 | Set up databases, fetch secrets |
| `PostPhase` | After all iterations complete | Run test suite, notify Slack |
| `PreIteration` | Before each Claude invocation | Inject context, update prompts |
| `PostIteration` | After each Claude response is parsed | Log token usage, trigger CI |
| `OnFailure` | When budget is exhausted without a promise | Alert on-call, open incident |
| `OnApproval` | When the approval gate presents a prompt | LLM-assisted decision making |

### Permission Mode Lifecycle Differences

| Mode | Phase approval | Iteration approval | File writes |
|------|---------------|-------------------|-------------|
| `strict` | Interactive prompt | Interactive prompt | Allowed |
| `standard` | Auto if ≤ threshold file changes | Auto-continue | Allowed |
| `autonomous` | Auto-approve | Auto-continue; prompt if 3+ stale iters | Allowed |
| `readonly` | Auto-approve | Auto-continue | **Blocked** |

### Promise Detection

Claude signals phase completion by emitting:

```xml
<promise>DONE</promise>
```

The token inside the tags must match the phase's configured `promise` string (case-sensitive). Once detected, the orchestrator stops iterations and advances to the next phase.

## Skills System

Skills are reusable prompt fragments in `.forge/skills/`:

```
.forge/skills/
├── rust-conventions/
│   └── SKILL.md
├── testing-strategy/
│   └── SKILL.md
└── api-design/
    └── SKILL.md
```

Reference skills in phases:

```json
{
  "number": "03",
  "name": "API implementation",
  "skills": ["rust-conventions", "api-design"],
  "promise": "API COMPLETE"
}
```

## Permission Modes

| Mode | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `strict` | Require approval for every iteration |
| `standard` | Approve phase start, auto-continue iterations |
| `autonomous` | Auto-approve if within budget and making progress |
| `readonly` | Research/planning only, no file modifications |

## Progress Signaling

Beyond binary promise detection, Claude can output intermediate signals:

```xml
<progress>50%</progress>     <!-- Partial completion -->
<blocker>Need X</blocker>    <!-- Pause and prompt user -->
<pivot>New approach Y</pivot> <!-- Strategy shift logged -->
```

## Sub-Phase Delegation

Phases can spawn child phases for discovered scope:

```xml
<spawn_subphase>
{
  "name": "Set up OAuth provider",
  "promise": "OAUTH COMPLETE",
  "budget": 5,
  "reasoning": "OAuth setup is complex enough for its own phase"
}
</spawn_subphase>
```

Sub-phases:
- Inherit parent's git snapshot as baseline
- Have independent budgets carved from parent
- Must complete before parent can complete
- Maintain hierarchical audit trail

## Context Compaction

Forge tracks context usage and automatically summarizes prior iterations when approaching limits:

- Preserves: current phase goal, recent code changes, error context
- Discards: verbose intermediate outputs, superseded attempts
- Configurable threshold via `--context-limit` or `forge.toml`

## Pattern Learning

Capture project patterns for future use:

```bash
# After completing a project
forge learn --name my-pattern

# Apply to new projects
forge patterns recommend --spec ./new-project-spec.md
```

Patterns capture:
- Phase type classification (scaffold, implement, test, refactor, fix)
- Typical iteration counts by phase type
- Common failure modes and recovery patterns
- File change patterns
- Effective prompt structures

## Directory Structure

### Project Directory

```
.forge/
├── forge.toml       # Configuration (optional)
├── hooks.toml       # Hooks (optional, can also be in forge.toml)
├── spec.md          # Project specification
├── phases.json      # Generated phases with dependencies
├── state            # Execution state (append-only)
├── checkpoints/     # Swarm checkpoint files for recovery
├── audit/
│   ├── runs/        # Completed run logs (JSON)
│   └── current-run.json
├── logs/            # Phase prompts and outputs
├── prompts/         # Custom prompt overrides
└── skills/          # Reusable prompt fragments
    ├── skill-name/
    │   └── SKILL.md
    └── ...
```

### Source Code Structure

```
src/
├── main.rs              # CLI entry point
├── lib.rs               # Library exports
├── phase.rs             # Phase definitions
├── forge_config.rs      # Configuration parsing
│
├── orchestrator/        # Core orchestration
│   ├── runner.rs        # Phase execution loop
│   ├── state.rs         # State persistence
│   └── review_integration.rs
│
├── dag/                 # DAG scheduler (swarm)
│   ├── builder.rs       # Graph construction
│   ├── scheduler.rs     # Wave computation
│   ├── executor.rs      # Parallel dispatch
│   └── state.rs         # Execution tracking
│
├── swarm/               # Swarm integration
│   ├── executor.rs      # Swarm orchestration
│   ├── context.rs       # Swarm types
│   ├── callback.rs      # HTTP callback server
│   └── prompts.rs       # Orchestration prompts
│
├── review/              # Review system
│   ├── specialists.rs   # Specialist definitions
│   ├── dispatcher.rs    # Review coordination
│   ├── arbiter.rs       # LLM resolution
│   └── findings.rs      # Finding types
│
├── decomposition/       # Dynamic decomposition
│   ├── config.rs        # Decomposition configuration
│   ├── detector.rs      # Complexity detection
│   ├── parser.rs        # Decomposition parsing
│   ├── executor.rs      # Sub-task execution
│   └── types.rs         # Decomposition types
│
├── factory/             # Factory Kanban board
│   ├── api.rs           # REST API handlers (Axum)
│   ├── server.rs        # Server startup & config
│   ├── db.rs            # SQLite database layer
│   ├── models.rs        # Data models & view types
│   ├── pipeline.rs      # Pipeline execution engine
│   └── ws.rs            # WebSocket message types
│
├── hooks/               # Hook system
├── skills/              # Skills management
├── context/             # Context management
├── tracker/             # Git operations
└── ui/                  # Progress display
```

## Example Session

```bash
$ forge init
Initialized forge project at .forge/

$ forge interview
# Interactive Q&A to generate spec...
Spec saved to .forge/spec.md

$ forge generate
Analyzing spec...
Generated 10 phases

$ forge run
[Phase 01/10] Project scaffolding
  Promise: SCAFFOLD COMPLETE | Budget: 8 iterations | Mode: standard

? Approve phase 01?
❯ Yes, run this phase
  Yes, and auto-approve remaining
  Skip this phase
  Abort

[Iteration 1/8] Running Claude...
  Context: 45% used
  Files: +15 added, 2 modified
✅ Promise found! Phase 01 complete.

[Phase 02/10] Core implementation
  Loading skills: rust-conventions
...

$ forge status
Forge Project Status
====================
Project: Initialized
Spec:    Ready
Phases:  Ready (10 phases)

Execution Progress:
  Phases completed: 5
  Last completed: 05

$ forge patterns
No patterns found. Complete a project and run 'forge learn'.
```

## Swarm Orchestration

Forge supports parallel phase execution through a hybrid swarm architecture that combines native Rust DAG scheduling with Claude Code's agent coordination capabilities.

### Overview

The swarm system provides:

- **Parallel Phase Execution**: Run independent phases simultaneously via DAG scheduling
- **Review Specialists**: Automated code review with security, performance, and architecture analysis
- **Dynamic Decomposition**: Automatically split complex phases into smaller tasks
- **LLM Arbiter**: Autonomous resolution of review failures without human intervention
- **Checkpoint Recovery**: Resume interrupted runs from saved state

### Swarm Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `forge swarm` | Execute phases in parallel using DAG scheduler |
| `forge swarm --from 05` | Start parallel execution from phase 05 |
| `forge swarm --only 07` | Run only phase 07 |
| `forge swarm status` | Show current swarm execution status |
| `forge swarm abort` | Gracefully stop running swarm |

### Swarm Options

| Option | Description | Default |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `--max-parallel <N>` | Maximum concurrent phases | 4 |
| `--backend <TYPE>` | Execution backend: auto, in-process, tmux, iterm2 | auto |
| `--review <SPECIALISTS>` | Enable review: security, performance, architecture, simplicity, all | none |
| `--review-mode <MODE>` | Resolution mode: manual, auto, arbiter | manual |
| `--max-fix-attempts <N>` | Maximum auto-fix attempts | 2 |
| `--escalate-on <TYPES>` | Always escalate these finding types (comma-separated) | none |
| `--arbiter-confidence <N>` | Minimum confidence for arbiter (0.0-1.0) | 0.7 |
| `--decompose` | Enable dynamic decomposition | true |
| `--no-decompose` | Disable dynamic decomposition | |
| `--decompose-threshold <N>` | Budget percentage to trigger decomposition | 50 |
| `--permission-mode <MODE>` | Permission mode: strict, standard, autonomous | standard |
| `--ui <MODE>` | Output format: full, minimal, json | full |
| `--fail-fast` | Stop all phases on first failure | disabled |

### How DAG Scheduling Works

Forge analyzes phase dependencies and computes execution waves:

```
phases.json with dependencies:
  01 (no deps) ─┐
  02 → [01]     │ Wave 1: [01]
  03 → [01]     ├ Wave 2: [02, 03, 04]
  04 → [01]     │ Wave 3: [05, 06]
  05 → [02, 03] │ Wave 4: [07]
  06 → [03, 04] │
  07 → [05, 06] ┘

Phases in the same wave run in parallel (up to --max-parallel).
```

### Phase Dependencies

Define dependencies in `phases.json`:

```json
{
  "number": "05",
  "name": "OAuth integration",
  "promise": "OAUTH COMPLETE",
  "budget": 10,
  "depends_on": ["02", "03"]
}
```

### Review Specialists

Review specialists automatically analyze completed phases:

| Specialist | Focus Areas | Gate |
|------------|-------------|------|
| `security` | Injection risks, auth issues, secrets exposure | Yes |
| `performance` | N+1 queries, memory leaks, algorithmic complexity | No |
| `architecture` | SOLID violations, coupling, separation of concerns | Yes |
| `simplicity` | Over-engineering, premature abstraction, YAGNI | No |

**Gating reviews** block phase completion until issues are resolved.
**Advisory reviews** report findings but don't block progress.

Enable reviews:

```bash
# Single specialist
forge swarm --review security

# Multiple specialists
forge swarm --review security,performance

# All specialists
forge swarm --review all
```

### Review Output

```json
{
  "phase": "05",
  "reviewer": "security-sentinel",
  "verdict": "warn",
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "warning",
      "file": "src/auth/oauth.rs",
      "line": 142,
      "issue": "Token stored in localStorage is vulnerable to XSS",
      "suggestion": "Use httpOnly cookies instead"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Resolution Modes

When a gating review fails, three resolution modes are available:

| Mode | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| `manual` | Always pause for user input |
| `auto` | Attempt auto-fix, retry up to 2 times |
| `arbiter` | LLM decides based on severity and context |

The **arbiter** mode uses an LLM to analyze findings and decide:

- **PROCEED**: Continue despite findings (style issues, false positives)
- **FIX**: Spawn fix agent and retry (clear fix path exists)
- **ESCALATE**: Require human decision (architectural concerns)

```bash
# Use arbiter with 80% confidence threshold
forge swarm --review security --review-mode arbiter --arbiter-confidence 0.8
```

### Dynamic Decomposition

When a phase is too complex, Forge can automatically decompose it:

**Triggers:**
- Worker emits `<blocker>` with complexity signal
- Iterations exceed 50% budget with progress < 30%
- Worker requests: `<request-decomposition/>`

**Example decomposition:**

```
Phase 05: OAuth Integration (budget: 20)
         │
         │ Detected: "3 separate provider integrations needed"
         ▼
┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Decomposition produces:                │
│ ├── 05.1: Google OAuth (budget: 5) ─┐  │
│ ├── 05.2: GitHub OAuth (budget: 5) ─┼─ parallel
│ ├── 05.3: Auth0 OAuth  (budget: 5) ─┘  │
│ └── 05.4: Unified handler (budget: 3)  │
│           depends_on: [05.1-3]         │
└────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Swarm Configuration

Configure swarm behavior in `forge.toml`:

```toml
[swarm]
enabled = true
backend = "auto"                    # auto, in-process, tmux, iterm2
default_strategy = "adaptive"
max_agents = 5

[swarm.reviews]
enabled = true
specialists = ["security", "performance"]
mode = "arbiter"                    # manual, auto, arbiter

# Per-phase swarm overrides
[phases.overrides."*-complex"]
swarm = { strategy = "parallel", max_agents = 4 }

[phases.overrides."*-refactor"]
swarm = { strategy = "wave_pipeline", reviews = ["architecture"] }
```

### Per-Phase Swarm Config

Enable swarm execution for specific phases:

```json
{
  "number": "05",
  "name": "OAuth integration",
  "promise": "OAUTH COMPLETE",
  "budget": 15,
  "swarm": {
    "strategy": "parallel",
    "max_agents": 4,
    "reviews": ["security"]
  }
}
```

### Checkpoint & Recovery

Forge automatically checkpoints progress during swarm execution:

```bash
# Resume interrupted run
$ forge swarm
Detected incomplete run: forge-run-20260126-150322
  Progress: 14/22 phases
  Checkpoints: 3 phases resumable

Resume? [Y/n] y

Recovering...
  ✓ Loaded checkpoints
  ✓ Reconciled state
  ✓ Respawning 3 phases

Continuing from Wave 4...
```

### Example Swarm Session

```bash
$ forge swarm --review security --max-parallel 3

Analyzing phase dependencies...
  22 phases, 8 execution waves

Wave 1: [01] ████████████ 100%  (3 iterations)

Wave 2: [02] ████████░░░░  67%
        [03] ████████████ 100%
        [06] ████████████ 100%

Reviews for [03]:
  ✓ security-sentinel: PASS

Wave 2: [02] ████████████ 100%  (10 iterations)

Reviews for [02]:
  ⚠ security-sentinel: WARN (1 finding)
    └─ src/db/queries.rs:42 - Consider parameterized query

Continuing (non-gating)...

Wave 3: [04] ████░░░░░░░░  33%
        [05*] Starting swarm...
              └─ Spawning 3 agents for OAuth integration
        [07] ████████████ 100%

All phases complete!
  Total time: 12m 34s
  Phases: 22/22
  Reviews: 8 passed, 1 warning
```

### Troubleshooting

#### Swarm won't start

```
Error: No phases.json found
```

Run `forge generate` first to create phases from your spec.

#### Phase stuck in "Blocked" state

Check dependencies with `forge list --deps`. A phase stays blocked until all its dependencies complete successfully.

#### Review keeps failing

1. Check the review findings in the output
2. Try `--review-mode auto` to attempt automatic fixes
3. Use `--review-mode arbiter` for LLM-assisted resolution
4. Lower the gating threshold or remove the specialist from gating

#### Out of memory during parallel execution

Reduce `--max-parallel` to limit concurrent Claude instances:

```bash
forge swarm --max-parallel 2
```

#### Checkpoint recovery fails

Clear checkpoints and restart:

```bash
rm -rf .forge/checkpoints/
forge swarm
```

## Factory (Kanban Board)

Forge Factory is a web-based Kanban board for managing issues that can self-implement through the Forge orchestration engine. Launch it with:

```bash
forge factory
```

This starts a local web server (default port 3000) with a drag-and-drop Kanban board and real-time pipeline progress.

### Kanban Columns

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Backlog** | New issues waiting to be worked on |
| **Ready** | Issues prioritized and ready for pipeline execution |
| **In Progress** | Issues currently being implemented by a pipeline |
| **In Review** | Pipeline completed, PR created and awaiting review |
| **Done** | Issue resolved and merged |

Issues can be dragged between columns manually, or they move automatically during pipeline execution.

### Self-Implementing Issues

Clicking **"Run Pipeline"** on an issue triggers the full implementation lifecycle:

1. **Column moves to In Progress** — issue is picked up
2. **Git branch created** — `forge/issue-{id}-{slug}` branch for isolation
3. **Phases auto-generated** — issue description written to `.forge/spec.md`, then `forge generate` creates `phases.json`
4. **Pipeline executes** — `forge swarm` runs if phases exist, otherwise falls back to `claude --print` for simple issues
5. **PR created** — on success, a pull request is opened via `gh pr create`
6. **Column moves to In Review** — signals the work is ready for human review

### Real-Time Progress

The Factory UI receives live updates via WebSocket:

| Event | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `PipelineStarted` | Pipeline execution begins |
| `PipelineBranchCreated` | Git branch created for the issue |
| `PipelinePhaseStarted` | A DAG phase begins execution |
| `PipelinePhaseCompleted` | A DAG phase finishes |
| `PipelineReviewStarted` | Code review begins for a phase |
| `PipelineReviewCompleted` | Code review finishes with findings |
| `PipelinePrCreated` | Pull request created on success |
| `PipelineCompleted` | Pipeline finishes (success or failure) |

The issue detail panel shows a **Phase Timeline** with per-phase status, iteration progress, and duration.

### Factory Options

| Option | Description | Default |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `--port <PORT>` | HTTP server port | `3000` |
| `--db-path <PATH>` | SQLite database file | `.forge/factory.db` |

### Factory REST API

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| `GET` | `/api/board` | Get board view with all columns and issues |
| `POST` | `/api/issues` | Create a new issue |
| `GET` | `/api/issues/:id` | Get issue detail with pipeline runs and phases |
| `PUT` | `/api/issues/:id` | Update an issue |
| `DELETE` | `/api/issues/:id` | Delete an issue |
| `PUT` | `/api/issues/:id/move` | Move issue to a different column |
| `POST` | `/api/issues/:id/pipeline` | Trigger pipeline execution |
| `POST` | `/api/pipeline-runs/:id/cancel` | Cancel a running pipeline |
| `GET` | `/ws` | WebSocket endpoint for real-time updates |

## Testing

```bash
# Run all tests
cargo test

# Unit tests only
cargo test --lib

# Integration tests only
cargo test --test integration_tests
```

## License

MIT

file.content:
[package]
name = "forge"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
description = "AI-powered development orchestrator - execute specs through sequential phases"

[dependencies]
# CLI
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
dialoguer = "0.12"
console = "0.16"
indicatif = "0.18"

# Async
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full", "process"] }
futures = "0.3"
futures-util = "0.3"

# HTTP Server (for swarm callback server)
axum = { version = "0.8", features = ["ws"] }
tower-http = { version = "0.6", features = ["cors"] }
open = "5"
reqwest = { version = "0.13", features = ["json", "form", "query"] }

# Git
git2 = "0.20.4"

# Serialization
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"

# Utilities
anyhow = "1"
thiserror = "2"
chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }
uuid = { version = "1", features = ["v4", "serde"] }
sha2 = "0.10"
walkdir = "2"
glob = "0.3"
dirs = "6"
toml = "1"
regex = "1"
textwrap = "0.16"
terminal_size = "0.4"
libsql = "0.9"
dotenvy = "0.15"
rust-embed = { version = "8", features = ["interpolate-folder-path"] }
mime_guess = "2"
bollard = "0.20"
async-trait = "0.1"

# Observability
tracing = "0.1"
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter", "json", "fmt"] }
tracing-appender = "0.2"
tracing-opentelemetry = { version = "0.28", optional = true }
opentelemetry = { version = "0.27", optional = true }
opentelemetry-otlp = { version = "0.27", optional = true }
opentelemetry_sdk = { version = "0.27", optional = true }

[features]
default = []
otlp = ["dep:tracing-opentelemetry", "dep:opentelemetry", "dep:opentelemetry-otlp", "dep:opentelemetry_sdk"]

[dev-dependencies]
tempfile = "3"
assert_cmd = "2"
predicates = "3"
tower = { version = "0.5", features = ["util"] }
http-body-util = "0.1"

1→use anyhow::{Context, Result};
     2→use clap::{Parser, Subcommand};
     3→use std::path::PathBuf;
     4→use tracing_subscriber::EnvFilter;
     5→
     6→mod cmd;
     7→
     8→#[derive(Parser)]
     9→#[command(name = "forge")]
    10→#[command(version, about = "AI-powered development orchestrator")]
    11→pub struct Cli {
    12→    #[arg(short, long, global = true)]
    13→    pub verbose: bool,
    14→
    15→    #[arg(long, global = true)]
    16→    pub yes: bool,
    17→
    18→    #[arg(long, default_value = "5", global = true)]
    19→    pub auto_approve_threshold: usize,
    20→
    21→    #[arg(long, global = true)]
    22→    pub project_dir: Option<PathBuf>,
    23→
    24→    /// Path to the spec file. If not provided, will search for *spec*.md files in docs/plans/
    25→    #[arg(long, global = true)]
    26→    pub spec_file: Option<PathBuf>,
    27→
    28→    /// Context limit for compaction (e.g., "80%" or "500000" chars). Overrides forge.toml setting.
    29→    #[arg(long, global = true)]
    30→    pub context_limit: Option<String>,
    31→
    32→    /// Enable autonomous mode for this run (overrides forge.toml).
    33→    #[arg(long, global = true)]
    34→    pub autonomous: bool,
    35→
    36→    /// Log level override (trace, debug, info, warn, error)
    37→    #[arg(long, global = true)]
    38→    pub log_level: Option<String>,
    39→
    40→    /// Log format override (json, compact)
    41→    #[arg(long, global = true)]
    42→    pub log_format: Option<forge::telemetry::LogFormat>,
    43→
    44→    /// OpenTelemetry OTLP endpoint for trace export
    45→    #[arg(long, global = true)]
    46→    pub otlp_endpoint: Option<String>,
    47→
    48→    #[command(subcommand)]
    49→    pub command: Commands,
    50→}
    51→
    52→#[derive(Subcommand)]
    53→pub enum Commands {
    54→    /// Initialize a new forge project
    55→    Init {
    56→        /// Copy from a pattern template
    57→        #[arg(long)]
    58→        from: Option<String>,
    59→    },
    60→    /// Conduct an interactive interview to generate a project spec
    61→    Interview,
    62→    /// Generate implementation phases from the project spec
    63→    Generate,
    64→    Run {
    65→        #[arg(short, long)]
    66→        phase: Option<String>,
    67→    },
    68→    Phase {
    69→        number: String,
    70→    },
    71→    List,
    72→    Status,
    73→    Reset {
    74→        #[arg(long)]
    75→        force: bool,
    76→    },
    77→    Audit {
    78→        #[command(subcommand)]
    79→        command: AuditCommands,
    80→    },
    81→    /// Learn a pattern from the current project
    82→    Learn {
    83→        /// Name for the pattern (defaults to directory name)
    84→        #[arg(short, long)]
    85→        name: Option<String>,
    86→    },
    87→    /// List or show learned patterns
    88→    Patterns {
    89→        #[command(subcommand)]
    90→        command: Option<PatternsCommands>,
    91→    },
    92→    /// View or validate configuration
    93→    Config {
    94→        #[command(subcommand)]
    95→        command: Option<ConfigCommands>,
    96→    },
    97→    /// Manage skills (reusable prompt fragments)
    98→    Skills {
    99→        #[command(subcommand)]
   100→        command: Option<SkillsCommands>,
   101→    },
   102→    /// Manually trigger context compaction for a phase
   103→    Compact {
   104→        /// Phase number to compact (defaults to current running phase)
   105→        #[arg(short, long)]
   106→        phase: Option<String>,
   107→        /// Show compaction status without performing compaction
   108→        #[arg(long)]
   109→        status: bool,
   110→    },
   111→    /// Implement a design document end-to-end with TDD phases
   112→    Implement {
   113→        /// Path to the design document (markdown)
   114→        design_doc: PathBuf,
   115→
   116→        /// Skip TDD test phase generation
   117→        #[arg(long)]
   118→        no_tdd: bool,
   119→
   120→        /// Start from a specific phase (for resuming)
   121→        #[arg(long)]
   122→        start_phase: Option<String>,
   123→
   124→        /// Generate spec and phases without executing
   125→        #[arg(long)]
   126→        dry_run: bool,
   127→    },
   128→    /// Launch the Code Factory Kanban UI
   129→    Factory {
   130→        /// Port to serve on
   131→        #[arg(short, long, default_value = "3141")]
   132→        port: u16,
   133→
   134→        /// Initialize database only (don't start server)
   135→        #[arg(long)]
   136→        init: bool,
   137→
   138→        /// Database path
   139→        #[arg(long, default_value = ".forge/factory.db")]
   140→        db_path: String,
   141→
   142→        /// Auto-open browser after server starts
   143→        #[arg(long, default_value = "true")]
   144→        open: bool,
   145→
   146→        /// Enable dev mode (CORS permissive for local Vite dev server)
   147→        #[arg(long)]
   148→        dev: bool,
   149→    },
   150→    /// Execute phases in parallel using DAG scheduling with optional reviews
   151→    Swarm {
   152→        #[command(subcommand)]
   153→        command: Option<SwarmCommands>,
   154→
   155→        /// Start from a specific phase
   156→        #[arg(long)]
   157→        from: Option<String>,
   158→
   159→        /// Run only these phases (comma-separated)
   160→        #[arg(long)]
   161→        only: Option<String>,
   162→
   163→        /// Maximum concurrent phases
   164→        #[arg(long, default_value = "4")]
   165→        max_parallel: usize,
   166→
   167→        /// Backend for swarm execution: auto, in-process, tmux, iterm2
   168→        #[arg(long, default_value = "auto")]
   169→        backend: String,
   170→
   171→        /// Enable review specialists (comma-separated: security,performance,architecture,simplicity,all)
   172→        #[arg(long)]
   173→        review: Option<String>,
   174→
   175→        /// Review mode: manual, auto, arbiter
   176→        #[arg(long, default_value = "manual")]
   177→        review_mode: String,
   178→
   179→        /// Maximum auto-fix attempts
   180→        #[arg(long, default_value = "2")]
   181→        max_fix_attempts: u32,
   182→
   183→        /// Always escalate these finding types (comma-separated)
   184→        #[arg(long)]
   185→        escalate_on: Option<String>,
   186→
   187→        /// Minimum arbiter confidence threshold (0.0-1.0)
   188→        #[arg(long, default_value = "0.7")]
   189→        arbiter_confidence: f64,
   190→
   191→        /// Enable dynamic decomposition (default)
   192→        #[arg(long, default_value = "true")]
   193→        decompose: bool,
   194→
   195→        /// Disable dynamic decomposition
   196→        #[arg(long)]
   197→        no_decompose: bool,
   198→
   199→        /// Budget percentage threshold to trigger decomposition
   200→        #[arg(long, default_value = "50")]
   201→        decompose_threshold: u32,
   202→
   203→        /// Permission mode: strict, standard, autonomous
   204→        #[arg(long, default_value = "standard")]
   205→        permission_mode: String,
   206→
   207→        /// UI output mode: full, minimal, json
   208→        #[arg(long, default_value = "full")]
   209→        ui: String,
   210→
   211→        /// Stop all phases on first failure
   212→        #[arg(long)]
   213→        fail_fast: bool,
   214→    },
   215→}
   216→
   217→#[derive(Subcommand, Clone)]
   218→pub enum SwarmCommands {
   219→    /// Show current swarm execution status
   220→    Status,
   221→    /// Gracefully abort the running swarm
   222→    Abort,
   223→}
   224→
   225→#[derive(Subcommand)]
   226→pub enum AuditCommands {
   227→    Show { phase: String },
   228→    Export { output: PathBuf },
   229→    Changes,
   230→}
   231→
   232→#[derive(Subcommand, Clone)]
   233→pub enum PatternsCommands {
   234→    /// Show details of a specific pattern
   235→    Show { name: String },
   236→    /// Show aggregate statistics by phase type across all patterns
   237→    Stats,
   238→    /// Recommend patterns for a spec file
   239→    Recommend {
   240→        /// Path to the spec file (defaults to .forge/spec.md)
   241→        #[arg(short, long)]
   242→        spec: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
   243→    },
   244→    /// Compare two patterns
   245→    Compare {
   246→        /// First pattern name
   247→        pattern1: String,
   248→        /// Second pattern name
   249→        pattern2: String,
   250→    },
   251→}
   252→
   253→#[derive(Subcommand, Clone)]
   254→pub enum ConfigCommands {
   255→    /// Show current configuration
   256→    Show,
   257→    /// Validate configuration and show any warnings
   258→    Validate,
   259→    /// Initialize a default forge.toml file
   260→    Init,
   261→}
   262→
   263→#[derive(Subcommand, Clone)]
   264→pub enum SkillsCommands {
   265→    /// List all available skills
   266→    List,
   267→    /// Show the content of a skill
   268→    Show { name: String },
   269→    /// Create a new skill
   270→    Create {
   271→        /// Name of the skill to create
   272→        name: String,
   273→        /// Path to a file containing the skill content (or stdin if not provided)
   274→        #[arg(short, long)]
   275→        file: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
   276→    },
   277→    /// Delete a skill
   278→    Delete {
   279→        /// Name of the skill to delete
   280→        name: String,
   281→        /// Skip confirmation prompt
   282→        #[arg(long)]
   283→        force: bool,
   284→    },
   285→}
   286→
   287→#[tokio::main]
   288→async fn main() -> Result<()> {
   289→    tracing_subscriber::fmt()
   290→        .with_env_filter(EnvFilter::from_default_env())
   291→        .with_target(false)
   292→        .with_thread_ids(false)
   293→        .init();
   294→
   295→    let cli = Cli::parse();
   296→    let project_dir = match cli.project_dir.clone() {
   297→        Some(dir) => dir,
   298→        None => std::env::current_dir().context("Failed to get current directory")?,
   299→    };
   300→
   301→    // Initialize telemetry (logging + tracing)
   302→    let log_level = cli
   303→        .log_level
   304→        .clone()
   305→        .or_else(|| std::env::var("FORGE_LOG").ok())
   306→        .unwrap_or_else(|| {
   307→            if cli.verbose {
   308→                "debug".to_string()
   309→            } else {
   310→                "warn".to_string()
   311→            }
   312→        });
   313→    let log_format = cli.log_format.clone().or_else(|| {
   314→        std::env::var("FORGE_LOG_FORMAT")
   315→            .ok()
   316→            .and_then(|s| match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {
   317→                "json" => Some(forge::telemetry::LogFormat::Json),
   318→                "compact" => Some(forge::telemetry::LogFormat::Compact),
   319→                _ => {
   320→                    eprintln!(
   321→                        "[forge] Warning: unknown FORGE_LOG_FORMAT '{}', ignoring",
   322→                        s
   323→                    );
   324→                    None
   325→                }
   326→            })
   327→    });
   328→    let otlp_endpoint = cli
   329→        .otlp_endpoint
   330→        .clone()
   331→        .or_else(|| std::env::var("FORGE_OTLP_ENDPOINT").ok());
   332→    let forge_dir = project_dir.join(".forge");
   333→    // Avoid creating `.forge/` as a side effect before `forge init` runs.
   334→    let log_dir = if forge::init::is_initialized(&project_dir) {
   335→        forge_dir.join("logs")
   336→    } else {
   337→        std::env::temp_dir().join("forge-logs")
   338→    };
   339→    if let Err(e) = forge::telemetry::init_telemetry(&forge::telemetry::TelemetryConfig {
   340→        log_level,
   341→        log_format,
   342→        log_dir,
   343→        otlp_endpoint,
   344→    }) {
   345→        eprintln!("[forge] Warning: failed to initialize telemetry: {:#}", e);
   346→    }
   347→
   348→    match &cli.command {
   349→        Commands::Init { from } => {
   350→            cmd::cmd_init(&project_dir, from.as_deref())?;
   351→        }
   352→        Commands::Interview => {
   353→            cmd::cmd_interview(&project_dir)?;
   354→        }
   355→        Commands::Generate => {
   356→            cmd::cmd_generate(&project_dir, cli.spec_file.as_deref(), cli.yes)?;
   357→        }
   358→        Commands::Run { phase } => {
   359→            cmd::run_orchestrator(&cli, project_dir, phase.clone()).await?;
   360→        }
   361→        Commands::Phase { number } => {
   362→            cmd::run_single_phase(&cli, project_dir, number).await?;
   363→        }
   364→        Commands::List => cmd::cmd_list(&project_dir)?,
   365→        Commands::Status => cmd::cmd_status(&project_dir)?,
   366→        Commands::Reset { force } => cmd::cmd_reset(&project_dir, &cli, *force)?,
   367→        Commands::Audit { command } => cmd::cmd_audit(&project_dir, command)?,
   368→        Commands::Learn { name } => cmd::cmd_learn(&project_dir, name.as_deref())?,
   369→        Commands::Patterns { command } => cmd::cmd_patterns(command.clone())?,
   370→        Commands::Config { command } => cmd::cmd_config(&project_dir, command.clone())?,
   371→        Commands::Skills { command } => cmd::cmd_skills(&project_dir, command.clone())?,
   372→        Commands::Compact { phase, status } => {
   373→            cmd::cmd_compact(&project_dir, &cli, phase.as_deref(), *status)?
   374→        }
   375→        Commands::Implement {
   376→            design_doc,
   377→            no_tdd,
   378→            start_phase,
   379→            dry_run,
   380→        } => {
   381→            if let Some(start) = start_phase {
   382→                // Verify phases exist before resuming
   383→                let phases_path = project_dir.join(".forge").join("phases.json");
   384→                if !phases_path.exists() {
   385→                    anyhow::bail!(
   386→                        "Cannot resume from phase {}: No phases.json found. Run 'forge implement <design-doc>' first.",
   387→                        start
   388→                    );
   389→                }
   390→                cmd::run_orchestrator(&cli, project_dir, Some(start.clone())).await?;
   391→            } else {
   392→                cmd::cmd_implement(&project_dir, design_doc, *no_tdd, *dry_run)?;
   393→            }
   394→        }
   395→        Commands::Factory {
   396→            port,
   397→            init,
   398→            db_path,
   399→            open,
   400→            dev,
   401→        } => {
   402→            let db_path = std::path::PathBuf::from(&db_path);
   403→            cmd::cmd_factory(*port, *init, db_path, *open, *dev).await?;
   404→        }
   405→        Commands::Swarm {
   406→            command,
   407→            from,
   408→            only,
   409→            max_parallel,
   410→            backend,
   411→            review,
   412→            review_mode,
   413→            max_fix_attempts,
   414→            escalate_on,
   415→            arbiter_confidence,
   416→            decompose,
   417→            no_decompose,
   418→            decompose_threshold,
   419→            permission_mode,
   420→            ui,
   421→            fail_fast,
   422→        } => {
   423→            // Handle subcommands first
   424→            if let Some(subcmd) = command {
   425→                match subcmd {
   426→                    SwarmCommands::Status => {
   427→                        cmd::cmd_swarm_status(&project_dir)?;
   428→                        return Ok(());
   429→                    }
   430→                    SwarmCommands::Abort => {
   431→                        cmd::cmd_swarm_abort(&project_dir)?;
   432→                        return Ok(());
   433→                    }
   434→                }
   435→            }
   436→
   437→            // Main swarm execution
   438→            cmd::cmd_swarm(
   439→                &project_dir,
   440→                &cli,
   441→                from.as_deref(),
   442→                only.as_deref(),
   443→                *max_parallel,
   444→                backend,
   445→                review.as_deref(),
   446→                review_mode,
   447→                *max_fix_attempts,
   448→                escalate_on.as_deref(),
   449→                *arbiter_confidence,
   450→                *decompose && !*no_decompose,
   451→                *decompose_threshold,
   452→                permission_mode,
   453→                ui,
   454→                *fail_fast,
   455→            )
   456→            .await?;
   457→        }
   458→    }
   459→
   460→    Ok(())
   461→}
   462→

content.text:
Here's how Forge works:

## Architecture Overview

Forge is a **Rust CLI tool** that orchestrates AI development by breaking project specs into executable phases, then running Claude CLI (`claude`) against each phase iteratively until completion.

## Core Flow

```
forge init → forge interview → forge generate → forge run/swarm
```

1. **`forge init`** — Creates `.forge/` directory structure
2. **`forge interview`** — Interactive Q&A to generate a project spec (`.forge/spec.md`)
3. **`forge generate`** — Analyzes the spec and produces `phases.json` with numbered phases, budgets, and dependencies
4. **`forge run`** — Executes phases sequentially, or **`forge swarm`** — executes in parallel via DAG scheduling

## Phase Execution Loop (`src/orchestrator/runner.rs`)

For each phase:
1. **Approval gate** — asks the user (or auto-approves based on permission mode)
2. **PrePhase hook** fires
3. **Iteration loop** (1 → budget):
   - Invokes Claude CLI with a structured prompt
   - Parses output for signals: `<promise>DONE</promise>` (completion), `<progress>`, `<blocker>`, `<pivot>`, `<spawn_subphase>`
   - Collects git diff for file change tracking
   - If promise found → phase complete; if budget exhausted → failure
4. **PostPhase hook** fires

## Key Subsystems

| Subsystem | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| **DAG Scheduler** (`src/dag/`) | Computes execution waves from phase dependencies for parallel execution |
| **Swarm** (`src/swarm/`) | Coordinates parallel phase execution with multiple Claude instances |
| **Review System** (`src/review/`) | Automated code review (security, performance, architecture, simplicity) with LLM arbiter resolution |
| **Decomposition** (`src/decomposition/`) | Auto-splits complex phases into sub-tasks when stuck |
| **Hooks** (`src/hooks/`) | Event-driven extensibility — command hooks (bash) or prompt hooks (LLM-evaluated) |
| **Compaction** (`src/compaction/`) | Summarizes prior iterations to prevent context overflow |
| **Factory** (`src/factory/`) | Web-based Kanban board UI with self-implementing issues — auto-branches, runs pipelines, creates PRs |
| **Patterns** (`src/patterns/`) | Learns from completed projects for future recommendations |
| **Skills** (`src/skills/`) | Reusable prompt fragments injected into phases |

## Two Execution Modes

- **Sequential** (`forge run`) — phases run one at a time via `src/orchestrator/runner.rs`
- **Parallel** (`forge swarm`) — DAG scheduler groups independent phases into waves, running up to `--max-parallel` concurrently

## Factory — The Full-Stack Feature

`forge factory` launches an Axum web server (port 3141) with a React Kanban UI. Issues dragged to "Ready" can trigger a full pipeline: branch creation → spec generation → phase generation → `forge swarm` execution → PR creation — all with real-time WebSocket progress updates.

## Permission Modes

| Mode | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| `strict` | Manual approval for every phase + iteration |
| `standard` | Auto-approve iterations, prompt for phase starts |
| `autonomous` | Fully automatic, only prompts if stale |
| `readonly` | Research only, no file writes |

The tool essentially wraps Claude CLI into a structured, phase-based execution engine with parallelism, reviews, and recovery — turning a spec document into a working codebase.
